paraphrase this into point form notes Cyberbullies can become bullies after being exposed to cyberbullying
Cyberbullies might have mental healts issues that contribute to their bullying or make it more severe
 Examples include problems with aggression, hyperactivity or impulsivity, as well as substance abuse.
In addition, those with personality features resembling the "dark tetrad" of narcissism or psychopathy may be at risk for cyberbullying.6 These individuals tend to have a low level of empathy for other people and may bully others as a way to increase their sense of power or worth.
Cyberbullies may also be people who struggle with feeling isolated or lonely in society.10 If they feel ignored by others, they may lash out as a way to feel better or vent their rage at society.

- Cyberbullies can turn into traditional bullies when they face cyberbullying themselves.
- Mental health problems may contribute to cyberbullying, such as aggression, hyperactivity, impulsivity, and substance abuse.
- People with narcissistic or psychopathic traits may be at higher risk of cyberbullying due to low empathy and a desire for power or worth.
- Cyberbullies may feel isolated and lonely, and bullying others can be a way to feel better or release their anger towards society.
paraphrase into point form notes
Sorry, I did not receive the original statement or text that needs to be paraphrased. Can you please provide the original statement or text so I can paraphrase it into point form notes?

paraphrase into point form notes Anonymity behind the screens
Bullies think that they’re protected if they use a fake name or account to attack others. This lack of accountability fosters an online culture allowing cyberbullying to flourish. 
Online bullies don’t get to see their victims’ reactions in real life so this can insulate them from the very real damage that they are doing.

- Cyberbullies feel protected behind fake names or accounts.
- The lack of accountability online allows cyberbullying to thrive.
- Without seeing their victims' reactions in real life, online bullies are insulated from the harm they cause.

paraphrase into point form notes ‘Cyberbullying’ is a range of behaviours that people use to intimidate, shame or alienate another person online. Cyberbullying includes, but is not limited to, using offensive or dehumanizing language against someone; blackmail; ‘outing’ someone; threatening physical violence. In the same way that bullies often try to isolate someone ‘in real life’ they might also try to rally others to isolate someone else in the online context and/or in ‘real life’.
Twitter, has enabled people to communicate with famous people in a way that wasn’t previously possible and there are many people who have used this opportunity to express their racist, misogynistic and homophobic beliefs directly at their target.
The internet also enables a person to bully strangers, without geographical limitations, via social media, comment sections, dating apps etc. in a way that wouldn’t have been possible previously.

- Cyberbullying involves various behaviors aimed at intimidating, shaming, or alienating someone online.
- Examples of cyberbullying include offensive language, blackmail, outing someone, and physical threats.
- Online bullies may try to isolate someone both online and in real life.
- Twitter allows people to directly communicate with celebrities, and some use it to express racist, homophobic, or misogynistic beliefs towards them.
- The internet allows bullies to target strangers from anywhere in the world through social media, comment sections, dating apps, etc.
create a 2 paragraph essay introduction introducing my hypothesis What are the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals engaging in cyber bullying/ online violence

Cyberbullying and online violence have become one of the most pressing issues of the Internet age. In recent years, the explosion of online communication technologies has given rise to new forms of harm inflicted on individuals in the virtual world. Regardless of its form, cyberbullying and online violence are always damaging to the victims, and have been linked to mental health problems, including anxiety, depression, and even suicide. 

In this essay, I will explore the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals engaging in cyberbullying and online violence. Specifically, I will examine how individual psychology, such as aggression, impulsivity, and low empathy, can be linked with this behavior. Additionally, I will explore how societal factors, including culture, family upbringing, and peer groups, can contribute to the prevalence of cyberbullying and online violence. Overall, by investigating the root causes of this dangerous phenomenon, we can take necessary measures to combat and reduce it.

create a 2 paragraph research report essay introduction introducing my hypothesis What are the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals engaging in cyber bullying/ online violence

Cyberbullying and online violence have become increasingly prevalent in modern society and have been linked to severe negative consequences for both victims and perpetrators. As the use of technology continues to grow and social media becomes more ubiquitous, it is crucial to understand the underlying causes of cyberbullying and online violence.

This research report aims to explore the psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals engaging in cyberbullying and online violence. Recent studies have shown that psychological traits like aggression, impulsivity, and low empathy can fuel this behavior, while societal factors like peer pressure, family dynamics, and cultural norms can also be contributing factors. By examining these factors, we can gain a better understanding of the root causes of cyberbullying and online violence and develop effective strategies to prevent and mitigate this harmful behavior. Ultimately, this study aims to improve the safety and wellbeing of individuals in the virtual world.
longer

Cyberbullying and online violence have become increasingly prevalent in modern society and have been linked to severe negative consequences for both victims and perpetrators. As the use of technology continues to grow and social media becomes more ubiquitous, it is crucial to understand the underlying causes of cyberbullying and online violence. 

Various studies have suggested that factors such as adolescent development, personality traits, and psychological disorders play a contributing role in the perpetration of cyberbullying and online violence. Additionally, societal factors such as cultural norms, socioeconomic status, family relationships, and peer pressures can also influence these behaviors. Understanding the interplay between these factors is vital to mitigating their harmful effects, as well as developing effective interventions to prevent their occurrence.

The psychological and emotional impacts that result from cyberbullying and online violence are significant and far-reaching. For victims, these experiences have been linked to anxiety, depression, self-harm behaviors, and even suicide. Meanwhile, perpetrators of cyberbullying and online violence are more likely to demonstrate maladaptive behaviors, including delinquency, substance abuse, and other conduct disorders. Therefore, there is a pressing need to identify the contributing factors to these behaviors, as well as to develop interventions that support the mental and emotional wellbeing of both victims and perpetrators. Through robust research and data collection, this report aims to advance our understanding of cyberbullying and online violence, ultimately promoting a healthier and safer online community.

create a 2 paragraph research report essay introduction introducing my hypothesis "What are the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals engaging in cyber bullying/ online violence"

Cyberbullying and online violence are becoming more prevalent and problematic in society, particularly as online communications continue to increase and social media influence expands. With cyberbullying and online violence having harmful effects on both victims and perpetrators, understanding the psychological and societal factors behind this behavior is critical for finding effective solutions and interventions.

In this research report, the underlying psychological and societal factors contributing to cyberbullying and online violence will be explored. By examining individual psychological traits such as empathy, aggression, and impulsivity, as well as societal factors including social norms, peer pressures, and family relationships, this report aims to provide insight into the complex nature of this behavior. Ultimately, the findings of this report may contribute to the development of effective interventions to combat and prevent cyberbullying and online violence, as well as promoting a safer and healthier online community for all.

Cyberbullying and online violence are two critical societal issues that need to be addressed. The increasing use of technology in our daily lives has made it easier for people to engage in bullying behavior anonymously, causing severe emotional and mental harm to the victims. As such, it is crucial to understand the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to cyberbullying and online violence.

This research report aims to delve deep into the root causes of cyberbullying and online violence by examining the various individual and societal factors that may fuel this behavior. By examining key psychological traits, such as aggression and low empathy, alongside societal factors such as culture, peer groups, and family dynamics, this report seeks to provide greater insight into this problematic issue. The ultimate goal of this research is to develop effective interventions and strategies to combat cyberbullying and online violence, making the online world a safer and more nurturing space for individuals to communicate.

how should i start an essay introduction

An essay introduction is crucial to a strong essay as it sets the tone for the entire piece of writing. A great introduction should grab the reader's attention, introduce the topic, provide background information, and succinctly state the thesis statement. Here are some potential ways to start an essay introduction:

1. Start with a rhetorical question: Begin with an intriguing question that will pique your reader's curiosity about the topic you are going to address. For example, "Did you know that over 60% of high school students report they have been bullied online?" 

2. Use a shocking statistic: Lead with a statistic that will grab your reader's attention and highlight the importance of your essay's topic. For example, "Every day, over 16,000 young people are absent from school due to bullying."

3. Share a personal anecdote: Draw your reader in by relating a personal story that is relevant to the topic you are addressing. This can create a relatable connection to your reader, highlighting the importance of the issue you are discussing.

4. Start with a quote: Use a well-known quote that is relevant to your topic to hook your reader into reading further. For instance, "To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment" by Ralph Waldo Emerson could hook your audience for an essay about authenticity and individuality. 

Whatever approach you use, remember that the introduction should entice the reader to continue reading, and set the stage for the central themes and ideas that you will explore in your essay.
rephrase About 37% of young people between the ages of 12 and 17 have been bullied online. 30% have had it happen more than once.

Online bullying is prevalent among young individuals aged between 12-17 years, with 37% reporting instances of bullying online. Furthermore, 30% of those individuals have experienced such behavior repeatedly.

Almost four out of ten young individuals aged between 12 and 17 have experienced online bullying, as per recent studies. Shockingly, these young people have shared that 30% of them have had to face the same traumatic experience again and again. In this research report I will be exploring media and cyberbullying and what the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals engaging in cyber bullying/ online violence.  The purpose of this study is to understand more deeply what the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals harassing others online. This research report will be closley tied with the social science of  psychology. Recent studies have found that nearly 40% of young people between the ages of 12 and 17 have been victims of online bullying, and 30% of them have experienced repeated instances of it. In this research report, I aim to explore the intersection of media and cyberbullying, deep diving into the underlying psychological and societal factors that contribute to individuals harassing others online.

My hypothesis is that several factors, including loneliness, mental illnesses, and a lack of in-person interaction, contribute to the widespread cases of cyberbullying in the present times. This research report will be anchored in the field of social science and psychology. Using a Google Form, I will collect primary data that supports my hypothesis, combining it with secondary research to build meaningful and reliable insights. The sample size will focus on young adults and teenagers, totaling thirty individuals, making the study nuanced and pertinent. 

The ultimate aim of this research is to draw attention to the consequences of cyberbullying and call for effective interventions. By examining the psychological and societal factors that contribute to this issue, we can craft measures that will foster a safer, healthier, and more inclusive online community.
in one paragraph

Online bullying is a pervasive issue affecting almost 40% of young individuals between 12-17 years of age, with 30% experiencing repeated instances, leading to significant mental, emotional and social effects. This research report aims to explore the factors contributing to cyberbullying, including loneliness, mental illnesses, and a lack of in-person interaction. The report is anchored in the social science and psychology fields and focuses on collecting primary data using a Google Form in association with secondary research to establish reliable insights. With a sample size of 30 young adults and teenagers, this study seeks to foster a safer and healthier online community by developing interventions that address the underlying psychological and societal factors contributing to the prevalence of cyberbullying.
